perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Remedies. The Receiving Party recognises and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is of a special, unique and extraordinary character to the Disclosing Party and the Target and the disclosure, misappropriation or unauthorised use of such Confidential Info rmation by the Receiving Party cannot be fully compensated and that, further, any such disclosure, misappropriation or unauthorised use of the Confidential Information shall cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and/or the Target. The Receiving Party expressly agrees, therefore, that the Disclosing Party, in addition to any rights and remedies it may have under this Agreement or at law or in equity, shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent the breach, or the further breach, of any of the provisions of this Ag reement. The Receiving Party agrees to reimburse the Disclosing Party for any and all losses, liabilities, damages, costs, opportunity costs, and direct or indi rect expenses (including reasonable attorney’s fees, profit opportunity returns foregone and any court costs) incurred by the Disclosing Party as a result of any prima facie breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.9 Storage of Confidential Information. 2.4 Remedies. The Receiving Party recognises and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is of a special, unique and extraordinary character to the Disclosing Party and the Target and the disclosure, misappropriation or unauthorised use of such Confidential Info rmation by the Receiving Party cannot be fully compensated and that, further, any such disclosure, misappropriation or unauthorised use of the Confidential Information shall cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and/or the Target. The Receiving Party expressly agrees, therefore, that the Disclosing Party, in addition to any rights and remedies it may have under this Agreement or at law or in equity, shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent the breach, or the further breach, of any of the provisions of this Ag reement. The Receiving Party agrees to reimburse the Disclosing Party for any and all losses, liabilities, damages, costs, opportunity costs, and direct or indi rect expenses (including reasonable attorney’s fees, profit opportunity returns foregone and any court costs) incurred by the Disclosing Party as a result of any prima facie breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Remedies' clause to be part of the 'Storage of Confidential Information' section buries it. This makes it more difficult for the Receiving Party to easily find and understand the full extent of their potential liabilities in case of a breach, potentially leading to enforceability issues.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Consumer Protection Act, 2008 (Act No. 68 of 2008) (South Africa)
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 22 of the Consumer Protection Act requires that contracts be written in plain and understandable language. Embedding the remedies clause within the section on storage of information obscures its importance, violating the requirement for clear and accessible terms.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the 'Remedies' clause makes it more difficult to find and understand potential liabilities, potentially leading to enforceability issues. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that Section 22 of the Consumer Protection Act requires contracts to be written in plain and understandable language, and that embedding the remedies clause obscures its importance. The Structural Flaws definition also includes instances where required legal disclosures are placed in irrelevant or misleading sections, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. While the provided law snippets are not helpful, the Law Explanation and perturbation details provide sufficient evidence that the moved clause creates a structural flaw that contradicts the principle of clear and accessible terms in contracts, specifically concerning remedies.
